fields.text: This French city is known globally as the 'City of Light' and features the Eiffel Tower.
This UK capital stands on the River Thames and contains the historic Tower of London.
This Italian city surrounds the independent enclave of Vatican City.
This city is the capital of Japan and is the most populous metropolitan area in the world.
This North American capital was built on the ruins of the Aztec capital, Tenochtitlan.
This large, black-and-white bear native to China eats almost exclusively bamboo.
This striped big cat is the largest of all the wild cat species.
This marine mammal is widely considered the largest animal ever known to have lived.
This flightless bird is the largest living bird species on Earth and can run up to 43 mph.
This slow-moving mammal spends most of its life hanging upside down in the rainforest canopy.
In 1997, Leonardo DiCaprio and Kate Winslet starred in this tragic romance set on a sinking ship.
This 1993 Steven Spielberg film brought cloned dinosaurs back to life on Isla Nublar.
This 2023 movie about the creator of the atomic bomb won the Oscar for Best Picture.
This director helmed the visually stunning sci-fi epics 'Inception' and 'Interstellar'.
Keanu Reeves plays a legendary assassin seeking vengeance for his dog in this action franchise.
Great houses clash for control of the Iron Throne in this hit HBO fantasy series.
Bryan Cranston played chemistry teacher turned drug lord Walter White in this drama series.
This Netflix sci-fi series features a group of kids in Hawkins, Indiana fighting the Upside Down.
Jeremy Strong and Brian Cox starred as members of the Roy family fighting for media control in this HBO drama.
This dystopian Hulu series is based on a classic 1985 novel by Margaret Atwood.
Introduced in ES6, this keyword allows you to declare a block-scoped variable that cannot be reassigned.
This built-in method is used to execute a function once for each element in an array.
This 3-letter acronym represents the programming interface for web documents that JavaScript manipulates.
This mechanism allows an inner function to access variables from its outer enclosing scope even after the outer function finishes.
This JavaScript keyword refers to the object that is currently executing the code block.
This standard HTML element is used to insert an image into a webpage.
This global attribute uniquely identifies an element within an HTML document.
This element tag is used to create a drop-down selection list in an HTML form.
This 4-letter attribute on an anchor tag specifies where to open the linked document, such as '_blank'.
Introduced in HTML5, this tag is used to embed independent, self-contained compositions like blog posts.
This 3-letter structured language is the standard used to manage and query relational databases.
This type of database key uniquely identifies each record in a specific table.
This popular open-source, NoSQL database stores data in flexible, JSON-like documents.
This database process removes data redundancy and ensures dependencies make sense across tables.
In ACID transactions, the letter 'C' stands for this property, ensuring data remains valid after a transaction.
Django is a high-level web framework written in this programming language.
This Django architectural pattern replaces the traditional 'Controller' with a Template.
This built-in Django command updates your database schema to match your current data models.
Django includes this functional component out-of-the-box to manage site content without building a custom backend interface.
This tool inside Django acts as a bridge between database tables and Python objects, removing the need to write raw SQL.
This CSS property is used to change the text color of an element.
This layout module provides an efficient way to align and distribute space among items in a single-dimensional container.
In the CSS Box Model, this area sits directly between the padding and the margin.
This CSS rule allows developers to apply styles only when certain device characteristics, like screen width, match.
This relative CSS unit is based on the font size of the root HTML element.
Ross, Rachel, and Monica drink coffee at Central Perk in this 90s comedy hit.
Michael Scott manages the Scranton branch of Dunder Mifflin in this mockumentary.
Jerry, George, Elaine, and Kramer complain about nothing in this iconic New York sitcom.
This animated comedy features a dad named Homer who works at a nuclear power plant.
Ted Mosby tells his children an incredibly long story about how he met their mother in this sitcom.
fields.answer: What is How I Met Your Mother?

fields.text: Luke Skywalker learns the ways of the Force to fight the Galactic Empire in this massive franchise.
Neo discovers reality is a simulated computer matrix in this 1999 cyberpunk film.
Timothée Chalamet plays Paul Atreides on the desert planet Arrakis in this Denis Villeneuve adaptation.
This 1982 Ridley Scott classic stars Harrison Ford as a cop hunting rogue replicants.
This 1968 Stanley Kubrick masterpiece features a sentient computer named HAL 9000.
Created by Facebook, this component-based UI library uses a virtual DOM.
This Google-backed framework uses TypeScript and is known for enterprise-scale web applications.
This runtime environment allows developers to execute JavaScript on the server side.
This progressive framework created by Evan You brands itself as approachable, versatile, and performant.
This lightweight compilation framework builds apps at build time rather than running a virtual DOM in the browser.
This HTML5 element is used to draw graphics on the fly via JavaScript scripting.
This storage API allows web applications to store data locally within the user's browser with no expiration date.
This API allows a web application to determine the user's physical location if permission is granted.
This script runs in the background, separate from the main browser thread, enabling offline capabilities and push notifications.
This API enables full, two-way, interactive communication sessions between a user's browser and a server.
This SQL command is used to extract data from a database.
This clause is used in SQL to filter records based on a specified condition.
This keyword is used to combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column.
This aggregate function returns the total number of rows that match a specified criterion.
This statement is used to arrange identical data into groups, often used with aggregate functions.
DRF uses these components to convert complex data like querysets into native Python datatypes that can easily be rendered into JSON.
This architectural style stands for Representational State Transfer and forms the basis of DRF APIs.
This class-based component in DRF combines the logic for a set of related views into a single class.
This specific type of token authentication is commonly used in DRF to handle secure, stateless user sessions via a signed string.
This type of class is used in DRF to restrict API access based on user status, like IsAuthenticated.
This property controls the stacking order of elements that overlap.
This keyword is used inside CSS declarations to give a property the highest priority, overriding all other selectors.
This layout system uses columns and rows to build complex two-dimensional web designs.
This property allows you to change the transparency level of an element.
Prefixing a variable name with these two characters defines a custom property in CSS.
Castaways attempt to 'Outwit, Outplay, Outlast' each other on a remote island in this long-running series.
Contestants hope to receive a roses from a single suitor in this romantic reality franchise.
Gordon Ramsay screams at chefs competing for a head chef position in this intense culinary show.
This show tracks home renovators buying, fixing, and reselling houses for a profit.
Drag queens showcase their charisma, uniqueness, nerve, and talent to win RuPaul's crown.
This giant prehistoric radiation-breathing monster is known as the 'King of the Monsters'.
This massive ape climbs the Empire State Building while swatting at airplanes.
Count Dracula is a famous movie monster belonging to this blood-sucking species.
This creature, built from old body parts, is brought to life by lightning in a classic 1931 horror film.
This terrifying shape-shifting entity takes the form of Pennywise the Dancing Clown.
This object represents the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation.
This modern keyword pair provides a cleaner syntax for working with Promises without chaining .then() blocks.
This global browser method is widely used to make network requests and returns a Promise.
This browser mechanism executes tasks, handles events, and processes queued sub-tasks in JavaScript.
This data structure holds asynchronous callbacks like setTimeout until the execution stack is empty.
Setting this attribute to 'password' on an input tag hides the typed characters from view.
This boolean attribute forces a user to fill out an input field before submitting a form.
This multi-line text input element allows for larger blocks of user text than a standard input tag.
This attribute associates a text label directly with an input field using the input's ID.
This form attribute specifies the HTTP method, such as GET or POST, used when sending data.
Unlike SQL, NoSQL databases generally do not use this rigid structure of rows and columns to organize data.
This highly popular, open-source NoSQL database functions as an in-memory key-value data store.
This type of NoSQL database uses nodes and edges to store relationships between data points.
This wide-column NoSQL database was originally developed by Facebook for inbox searches.
This foundational computer science theorem states a distributed database can only guarantee two out of Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance.
This fundamental setting variable must be set to False in a production Django application to avoid leaking error details.
This command gathers all your application's asset files into one production-ready directory.
This 4-letter acronym describes the standard web server interface used to run Python web applications like Django.
This production HTTP server engine is commonly paired with Gunicorn to serve static assets and reverse-proxy requests to Django.
This setting variable lists the specific host/domain names that the Django site can serve.
This highly popular CSS extension language stands for Syntactically Awesome Style Sheets.
Preprocessors must do this to your custom syntax files before a web browser can read them as standard CSS.
This feature allows you to clean up code by embedding CSS selectors inside one another to match your HTML hierarchy.
This preprocessor feature allows you to define groups of CSS declarations that you can reuse throughout your stylesheet.
This directive allows you to share a set of CSS properties from one selector to another, helping keep code DRY.
This built-in function returns the number of items in an object, such as characters in a string or elements in a list.
This concise syntax format provides a clean way to create new lists based on existing iterables, wrapping the logic inside square brackets.
Unlike languages that use curly braces, Python famously relies on this structural formatting requirement to define blocks of code.
This keyword is used inside a Python function to create a generator, returning values one at a time instead of all at once.
This 4-letter special method name, surrounded by double underscores, serves as the constructor to initialize new objects in a class.
Dexter hides a massive, high-tech facility from his parents right behind the bookcase in this room.
Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup are collectively known as these superpowered kindergarteners.
Finn the Human and Jake the Dog explore the post-apocalyptic Land of Ooo in this series.
This cowardly pink canine must constantly save his elderly owners, Muriel and Eustace, from paranormal threats.
fields.answer: Who is Courage the Cowardly Dog?

fields.text: This 10-year-old boy uses a mysterious alien watch called the Omnitrix to transform into different extraterrestrial heroes.
This optimistic yellow sea sponge lives in a pineapple under the sea in Bikini Bottom.
Tommy, Chuckie, Phil, and Lil look at the world from a toddler's perspective in this classic 90s Nicktoon.
Aang must master control over air, water, earth, and fire to defeat the Fire Nation in this highly acclaimed animated series.
fields.answer: What is Avatar: The Last Airbender?

fields.text: Timmy Turner relies on Cosmo and Wanda, his two green and pink floating helpers, in this magical cartoon.
This young boy can turn invisible and fly after a lab accident transforms him into a half-ghost superhero.
Miley Stewart lives a double life as a regular high school student and a global pop superstar in this hit show.
This animated series features two stepbrothers spending their 104 days of summer vacation building wild inventions like rollercoasters.
Twins Dipper and Mabel Pines spend their summer unravelling local paranormal mysteries in this Oregon town.
Alex, Justin, and Max Russo compete against each other to see who gets to keep their magical powers in this New York-set sitcom.
fields.answer: What is Wizards of Waverly Place?

fields.text: This teenage cheerleader fights global villains alongside her sidekick Ron Stoppable and a naked mole-rat named Rufus.
This iconic dog uses a mystery-solving machine alongside Fred, Daphne, Velma, and Shaggy to unmask spooky villains.
This classic cat-and-mouse duo spent decades violently outsmarting each other across various animated iterations.
Heads up! These radical, pizza-loving sewer reptiles named Leonardo, Raphael, Donatello, and Michelangelo fought crime in NYC.
fields.answer: What is Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles?

fields.text: This group of mutant superheroes led by Professor X fought for peace in a legendary 90s animated series featuring a heavy metal guitar theme song.
He-Man defended the secrets of Castle Grayskull from this blue-skinned, staff-wielding villain on the planet Eternia.
This bumbling first mate wears a red shirt and a white bucket hat while causing mishaps on the uncharted desert isle.
The opening theme song notes that the fateful trip of the S.S. Minnow was originally scheduled for this many hours.
Using little more than bamboo and coconuts, this brilliant academic crafts complex radios and gadgets for the castaways.
Thurston Howell III is a billionaire castaway who stranded himself on the island alongside this posh, nicknamed wife.
This glamorous Hollywood movie star stranded on the island is always seen in elegant evening gowns.
Lucy Ricardo is married to this fiery Cuban bandleader who frequently performs at the Tropicana club.
In an iconic episode, Lucy and Ethel lose control while working on a fast-moving assembly line making this sweet treat.
This health tonic commercial goes hilariously wrong when Lucy gets drunk off its high alcohol content while practicing her pitch.
Fred and Ethel, the Ricardos' close friends and landlords, have this last name.
Before moving to a country home in Connecticut, the Ricardos live in an apartment building in this borough of New York City.
Susan Lucci famously portrayed this legendary, multi-married grand dame of daytime television for over 41 years.
This fictional Pennsylvania suburb serves as the primary setting for the dramatic lives of the residents in this soap.
This patriarch and doctor, played by David Canary, had an identical, gentler twin brother named Stuart Chandler.
Before finding mega-stardom in Hollywood blockbusters like 'Black Panther', this actor played Reggie Montgomery on the show.
This daytime supercouple, played by Kelly Ripa and Mark Consuelos, eventually eloped in real life too.
fields.answer: Who are Hayley Vaughan and Mateo Santos?

fields.text: The long-running, fierce corporate and personal rivalry on this show centers around the Abbott family and this other wealthy family.
This fictional Wisconsin city serves as the industrial and social backdrop for the show's billionaires and working class.
Eric Braeden has spent decades portraying this ruthless, cigar-chomping tycoon patriarch of Newman Enterprises.
This cosmetics giant is the primary business empire operated by the Abbott family.
This iconic actress, who played the beloved, multi-married character Nikki Newman, first joined the soap way back in 1979.
Erika Slezak won a record six Daytime Emmy Awards playing Victoria, the matriarch of this powerful family.
The dramatic events of this soap unfold in this fictional, affluent Pennsylvania town situated on the Llanview River.
This villain-turned-antihero, played by Michael Easton, is a dark, brooding vampire who crossed over from the soap 'Port Charles'.
fields.answer: Who is Caleb Morley (or John McBain)?

fields.text: Before playing the MCU's Hawkeye, this actor spent the late 90s portraying the troubled teenager Jacked Apple.
Victoria Lord famously suffered from DID, a psychological condition known by this 3-letter acronym.
This legendary martial artist and internet meme icon starred as Cordell Walker in 'Walker, Texas Ranger'.
Walker's trusted, tech-savvy younger partner Jimmy Trivette previously played this position in the NFL before injuring his knee.
This retired Texas Ranger and seasoned mentor frequently gives Walker advice while cooking up grub at his local bar and grill.
Cordell Walker relies heavily on this specific, hard-hitting style of martial arts which Chuck Norris actually founded in real life.
In the 2021 television reboot simply titled 'Walker', this former 'Supernatural' star stepped into the titular boots.
In this high-concept Netflix sci-fi series, a hijacked airplane must constantly fly away from this cosmic threat to survive.
The initial hijacking of the commercial flight takes off from an international airport located in this European capital city.
This 2022 Turkish spin-off series focuses on a submarine crew navigating the exact same solar apocalypse.
The series is a thrilling adaptation based on 'The Old Axolotl', a digital sci-fi novel written by this Polish author.
To protect themselves from cosmic rays during a critical layover, the survivors seek shelter inside this specific type of underground military facility.
Wesley Snipes famously portrayed this leather-jacketed Marvel superhero who hunts vampires to avenge his mother.
Because he is a half-vampire who is completely immune to sunlight, vampires fearfully refer to Blade by this nickname.
This grizzled weapons smith and vampire hunter serves as Blade's mentor and surrogate father figure.
In the acclaimed 2002 sequel 'Blade II', this visionary director took over the helm to introduce a mutated breed of vampires called Reapers.
Blade wields a signature sword forged from this specific precious metal, which happens to be lethal to vampires.
David Hasselhoff plays Michael Knight, a high-tech crimefighter who drives an artificially intelligent, talking sports car named this.
KITT's physical chassis is a heavily modified version of this iconic black General Motors sports car model.
fields.answer: What is a Pontiac Firebird Trans Am?

fields.text: This private crime-fighting justice organization, funded by a wealthy billionaire, employs Michael and builds KITT.
fields.answer: What is FLAG (Foundation for Law and Government)?

fields.text: This actor provided the iconic, sophisticated, uncredited voice of KITT throughout the entire original series.
In season two, Michael faces off against this evil, mustache-twirling clone of himself, who drives an armored truck.
Lee Majors stars as this astronaut who is rebuilt with high-tech bionic implants following a catastrophic experimental plane crash.
Steve Austin works as a secret agent for this fictional government intelligence office, known by a 3-letter acronym.
fields.answer: What is the OSI (Office of Scientific Intelligence)?

fields.text: This popular 1976 spin-off series features Steve's injured tennis pro fiancée, Jamie Sommers, getting her own mechanical upgrades.
Steve frequently clashes with this massive, hairy cryptid, who turns out to be an alien android operating in the wilderness.
This visionary cybernetics doctor is the mastermind behind the surgical engineering that built Steve's bionic limbs.
This legendary comedian hosted a self-titled 90s sitcom about 'nothing' and is famous for asking 'What's the deal with...?'
This boundary-pushing comic behind 'Seven Words You Can Never Say on Television' was arrested in 1972 for disturbing the peace.
This fast-talking comic voiced the Genie in 'Aladdin' and got his big break doing manic stand-up routines in San Francisco.
This brilliant comic walked away from a $50 million contract for his sketch show to return to raw stand-up comedy and smaller venues.
This pioneering 1960s comic challenged American obscenity laws with his raw, satirical political routines before an early death in 1966.
